channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Robinhood halts Super Bowl betting contracts after CFTC request
item.description: Robinhood said on Tuesday it is rolling back the event contracts that would let users bet on the result of the Super Bowl clash this weekend, after the online brokerage received a request from the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Rust update fixes ‘forever’ compilation
item.description: The Rust Team has published a point release update, Rust 1.84.1, to address a few regressions in last month’s Rust 1.84 release, including fixing painfully slow compilation related to the next-generation trait solver.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SAP throws a lifeline to large organizations with new ECC offering
item.description: SAP on Tuesday said it plans to announce a new offering in the next quarter that is based around SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) and is designed to help the firm’s largest customers “plan, execute and succeed in their cloud transformation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hacked on social? Here’s what to do next
item.description: Spambots, data thieves, and other bad actors run rampant online — more so now than ever before. Here's what to do if one gets ahold of you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AMD’s big RX 9070 launch might happen later in February – and we could find out how these GPUs compare to the Nvidia RTX 5070
item.description: Are the RX 9070 graphics cards set to be Nvidia RTX 5070 killers? We need to know one key detail for sure: those crucial MSRPs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly available pornography app available in the European Union, and warned that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mystery OPM server that aided buyout offers prompts House Democrat probe
item.description: House Oversight Democrats are demanding answers about the installation of a “server of unknown nature and origin” at the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) that aided the agency in sending buyout offers to federal employees.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Phishing campaign targets prominent X users, accounts at risk
item.description: A phishing campaign is hijacking high profile X users and using their accounts for fraudulent activity.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta reveals what kinds of AI even it would think too risky to release
item.description: Meta wants to work with industry experts, governments, and others to stop AI from becoming a problem to society.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ransomware Attack Exposes 569,012 People's Sensitive Information - Healthcare Provider Under Scrutiny
item.description: NorthBay Health confirms data breach affecting 569,012 individuals' financial, medical, and health insurance details in a ransomware attack last year. The Embargo group is believed to be behind the incident.
item.dc:creator: Robin Miller, The Mercury News

item.title: The iPhone’s first official porn app has just landed in the EU – and Apple really isn’t happy about it
item.description: Apple says it's "deeply concerned about the safety risks" of a new porn app called Hot Tub that's just arrived on a third-party app store in the EU.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: As EV vehicles get heavier, they’re also getting more dangerous, safety experts say
item.description: the proliferation of heavier electric trucks in recent years has raised concerns among safety experts who fear their increased speed and weight will lead to deadlier incidents
item.dc:creator: Chase Hunter, The Mercury News

item.title: Gemini AI to Get More Contextual with Google Maps Integration
item.description: Gemini AI integration in Google Maps adds a new feature allowing users to ask about places on the map using the Gemini assistant.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sick of your MacBook starting when you open the lid? Apple has just revealed a fix
item.description: Apple has published a support article detailing how you can stop your MacBook from turning on when you open the lid.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: Former PlayStation boss is hoping for ‘something that is still hidden to us’ to be revealed in the Nintendo Switch 2 direct to make the console more exciting
item.description: A former PlayStation boss wasn’t surprised by the Nintendo Switch 2 reveal and hopes there’s something more exciting in store.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: Los CIO son optimistas respecto a los agentes de IA; los empleados de TI, no tanto
item.description: La mayoría de los directores de sistemas de la información (CIO, por sus siglas en inglés) y directores de tecnología (CTO) son optimistas respecto a los agentes de inteligencia artificial (IA), y creen que la tecnología emergente pronto será esencial para sus empresas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google Messages will get a big emergency texting upgrade soon –here's what's coming
item.description: Google has promised to support emergency texts over RCS, and it looks like a launch could be imminent.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to easily transfer files and photos off a computer to your hard drive
item.description: It's not just about freeing up space on your computer, Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says. It's about protecting your digital memories and important documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OpenAI and SoftBank announce artificial intelligence joint venture
item.description: Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU supports AI challenge to Silicon Valley and China
item.description: The European Union is backing a major AI initiative, OpenEuroLLM, in a bid to counter the dominance of Silicon Valley and China in artificial intelligence. The initiative is being led by Jan Hajič, a computational linguist at Charles University in Czechia, and Peter Sarlin, co-founder of Silo AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: JavaScript Temporal to ease dates and times
item.description: Web developers will soon find it easier to work with dates and times in JavaScript, thanks to emerging browser support for JavaScript’s Temporal object, according to Mozilla.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cybersecurity has had its ups and downs as we head into 2025
item.description: Last year was an eventful one on the cybersecurity front. But big cyber developments in the federal scape are expected in 2025. The post Cybersecurity has had its ups and downs as we head into 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: UnitedHealth cyberattack exposes 190 million in largest US healthcare data breach
item.description: The number of people affected by a UnitedHealth data breach in February 2024 was actually higher than previously reported and was the largest medical data breach in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
